instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Life Insurance For Heart Attack Victims
Cardiovascular conditions [1] are not only one of the leading causes of fatality around the world, but also a cause for concern in India. In the past decade, India was known to have the highest burden of cardiovascular diseases globally [2].
While it is important to take care of your health and put your wellbeing first by maintaining a healthy lifestyle, it is also necessary to make financial provisions for yourself and your family, should you have to face any dire situation tomorrow. This may become especially important if you have already experienced a heart attack, as your health may now considered to be even more fragile.
But why is life insurance for heart attack victims needed? One may think that the worst is behind them and that surviving a heart attack means that they are now better. However, that may be far from the truth. Remember that life is full of uncertainties. Simply because you survived one extreme incident does not mean that you may not have to face another one.
In fact, having faced a heart attack can leave you weaker. It may end up making your health more sensitive and in need of recovery. After going through a heart attack, you now have to be more careful and deliberate about your health.
Getting life insurance may not be your first thought after having gone through a heart attack, but life insurance for heart attack survivors may prove to be helpful. If you don’t already have one, it may be time to consider getting a life insurance plan.
Let’s see why this may be the case and how life insurance for heart attack victims is something that should be considered.
Why life insurance for heart attack victims is important
Before we dive into why heart attack survivors should get a life insurance plan, let’s take a look at what a heart attack is. Formally known as myocardial infarction, a heart attack is what happens when the muscles of the heart experience a lack of blood supply. [3] If not attended to immediately, it may end up worsening. [3]
One of the common causes of a heart attack is CAD or coronary artery disease. [3] It can also be caused due to arterial spasms or contractions. [3] Common symptoms observed are chest pain, discomfort or pain in the arm, shoulder, back, and/or neck, and shortness of breath. [3]
It is necessary to immediately go to a doctor or a hospital if you experience any such symptoms. [3] After you recover from a heart attack, it is important to start taking better care of yourself. For example, you should start making slow but steady lifestyle changes, such as eating healthy and exercising. [3]
The next step you may take is to prepare for the worst if you haven’t already done so. While it may sound scary or bleak, and thinking about it may be overwhelming, one should realise that it may be advisable to be prepared for the worst while hoping for the good and working towards it.
One of the steps to prepare for any adversity following a heart attack is to ensure the protection of your family and loved ones. This is why life insurance for heart attack survivors may be essential. It offers you a way to protect your near and dear ones with a blanket of financial security. Furthermore, if you opt for riders with your plan, such as a critical illness rider, you may also be able to protect yourself against any medical costs that you may face in the future due to healthcare concerns.
How to get life insurance for heart attack survivors?
One of the major differences between getting life insurance for a heart attack survivor versus someone who is relatively healthier is that the former may be required to undergo some additional tests or provide a declaration regarding their health.
Let’s take a look at how getting life insurance for a heart attack victim might be possible.
Choosing a plan
One of the first steps when buying life insurance for heart attack survivors may be the same as buying it for someone who hasn’t had one. You may start by looking at the plan options available and choose the ones that suit you. To understand which one is the right fit for you, define your needs and goals. For example, if you are looking for a relatively low-cost plan, term insurance may be your choice. However, if you want coverage for as long as possible, whole life plans may be the right option for you.
Deciding on the riders
When getting life insurance for heart attack victims, riders may tend to become an important part of the plan. Surviving a heart attack might make your health, in general, more susceptible to future complications. Riders may help you protect your savings against any future health emergencies. For example, a critical illness rider may help you protect yourself if you were to experience any serious health complications in the future.
Use a life insurance calculator
Before you buy a plan, ensure that you use a life insurance calculator to get estimates of how much you may be required to pay into the plan for the sum assured you would like as well as the term duration you seek. This may help you shortlist the plans and choose ones that seem affordable to you.
Buy the plan
This step can be completed online as well as offline. Buying life insurance plans online may be more cost-effective. You may consult an insurance agent or an insurer representative to understand the buying process as well. At the time of buying, ensure that you offer complete and accurate information about yourself and your health. A lack of full disclosure may lead to any claims in the future being rejected.
After having experienced a heart attack, it becomes more important to start taking care of your health and wellbeing. Also, buying life insurance for someone who has survived a heart attack may not only be possible, but it may also prove to be important in the future. Ensure that when buying the plan, you offer full disclosure to the life insurance plan provider.
Source links:
https://www.who.int/news-room/fact-sheets/detail/cardiovascular-diseases-(cvds)
https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3408699/
https://www.cdc.gov/heartdisease/heart_attack.htm
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
